game_name: Shining Force: The Legacy of Great Intention

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental mechanic established in tutorial-like scenarios
Resource management introduced as enemies deal damage
Players learn to build strategic team during peaceful village phases
Equipment and ability access tied to class; specialization decisions emerge
Stat rebalancing and progression complexity increases
Kill all enemies, kill leader, or advance to location; variety prevents repetition
Japanese-style RPG exploration for treasure, equipment, narrative
Ally positioning and terrain tactics become increasingly critical
Strategic focus shift from attrition to objective-based tactics

element_introduction_order.element: Dark Dragon revival and endgame complexity
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Runefaust forces, Kane, Darksol, and dark magic culminate in final threats
Merging light and dark swords; ultimate sealing ability against Dark Dragon

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to Kingdom of Guardiana and other towns between battles
Exploration mode interactions and NPC dialogue
Level-up and character stat increases after victory
typical_tension_triggers: Battle initiation with new enemy types
Encounters with Runefaust forces and evil Kane
HP loss and resource depletion during combat
Multiple objective battles requiring strategic positioning
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating cycle of tactical turn-based battle tension with peaceful exploration and preparation. Early game provides gentle challenge while mid-to-late game gradually escalates enemy difficulty and battle complexity. No time pressure within turns allows for deliberate strategic thinking.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Treasure within towns and exploration areas accessible through NPC interaction and navigation; optional characters recruitable during exploration phases

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction to grid-based movement and basic combat mechanics; small battlefields allow learning turn order, agility, and positioning without overwhelming complexity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive enemy variety and placement difficulty; class promotion unlocks new tactical options; multiple objective types introduced; multi-character coordination required
difficulty_curve.late_game: Darksol's manipulation of Kane and King Ramladu creates narrative and mechanical complexity; Dark Dragon resurrection represents peak difficulty; Chaos Breaker fusion mechanic becomes essential
notable_spikes: First encounter with superior enemy numbers requiring tactical positioning
Introduction of promotion system causing temporary stat reduction before power spike
Mid-game transition from simple elimination objectives to complex multi-stage battles
Darksol's revelation as true antagonist escalates magical threat complexity
Dark Dragon final battle requiring specific Chaos Breaker strategy

notable_level_patterns.description: Alternating exploration in peaceful Kingdom of Guardiana and affiliated towns with turn-based tactical battles. Towns provide safety for recruitment, equipment trading, and NPC narrative. Battles serve as challenge and advancement gates.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Kingdom of Guardiana exploration followed by first Runefaust engagement

notable_level_patterns.description: Exploration phases serve as recruitment windows where Max encounters potential allies with unique classes and backgrounds. Army composition directly impacts battle success and available tactical options.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early Kingdom of Guardiana recruitment sequence

notable_level_patterns.description: Battles progress from simple 'defeat all enemies' objectives to 'defeat leader only' and 'advance to landmark' variants. Each objective type demands different tactical approaches and unit prioritization.
Promotion system at levels 10-20 creates secondary objective of managing class advancement timing. Stat reduction during promotion creates risk-reward tension in combat preparation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-game battles coinciding with first character promotions
